items.q: Why was Vesalius significant in the history of medicine?
items.correct: His 1543 De Humani Corporis Fabrica corrected Galen's errors via human dissection and accurate illustration, founding modern anatomy though not curing disease.
items.misconception: Vesalius discovered the cause of disease and ended humoral theory.
items.why: Vesalius improved anatomy but disease theory still ran on humours for centuries. Students conflate progress in one area (structure) with progress in another (cause of illness).

items.q: Why was the founding of the NHS controversial in 1948?
items.correct: The British Medical Association opposed nationalisation of doctors; Bevan made concessions allowing private practice; Conservatives feared cost and central control.
items.misconception: The NHS was supported by all parties and all doctors from the start.
items.random_wrong: The NHS was originally only for soldiers returning from WWII.
items.why: Bevan said he had to 'stuff their mouths with gold' to get doctors on board. Students project today's cross-party support backwards onto a deeply contested founding.

items.q: How did the feudal system work in Norman England?
items.correct: The king granted land to tenants-in-chief in return for knight service; they in turn enfeoffed knights who held land in return for military service to their lord.
items.misconception: Feudalism meant peasants were slaves owned by the king.
items.random_wrong: Feudalism was a written constitution introduced by William in 1066.
items.why: Feudalism described a tenurial chain of obligations among free landholders, not slavery. Most peasants were villeins tied to manors — a related but separate system.

items.q: Who mapped the structure of DNA in 1953?
items.why: It's Watson and Crick. “Fleming and Florey” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Which 1942 report led to the creation of the NHS?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Beveridge Report. “Chadwick Report” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: In what year was the Public Health Act passed in Britain?
items.why: Exact answer: 1875. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
